claims.claim: ElevenLabs maps sample rate to format string pcm_8000/.../pcm_24000, default 24000
claims.found: def output_format_from_sample_rate(...) match sample_rate: case 8000: return 'pcm_8000' ... case 24000: return 'pcm_24000' ... return 'pcm_24000' (fallback at L149)

claims.claim: Twilio serializer calls pcm_to_ulaw(data, frame.sample_rate, 8000, resampler) on egress
claims.found: serialized_data = await pcm_to_ulaw(data, frame.sample_rate, self._twilio_sample_rate, self._output_resampler) — comment: 'Convert PCM at frame's rate to 8kHz μ-law for Twilio'
in_pcm_bytes = await resampler.resample(pcm_bytes, in_rate, out_rate) (L206); out_ulaw_bytes = audioop.lin2ulaw(in_pcm_bytes, 2) (L209)

claims.claim: ProcessOutputAudio -> adapter.ConvertOutput -> g711 lib resamples TTS PCM to mu-law 8kHz mono
claims.found: ProcessOutputAudio (L130) calls adapter.ConvertOutput (L135). But ConvertOutput (audio_adapter.go:43-45) does ONLY resampler.Resample(audio, Linear16kHz->Mulaw8kHz config) — mu-law conversion happens inside the resampler, NOT via g711. The g711 lib (zaf/g711 EncodeUlaw/DecodeUlaw) is used only in MixAmbient (audio_adapter.go:52-63), not in the main ConvertOutput output path.
claims.correction: g711 is in the file (audio_adapter.go:13,52,57,63) but only on the ambient-mix path; the main ConvertOutput path encodes mu-law via resampler.Resample to NewMulaw8khzMonoAudioConfig(). Broad claim (PCM->mu-law 8kHz mono) correct; g711 attribution to the main path is inaccurate.

claims.claim: Deepgram: telephony is '8 kHz mono mu-law or A-law PCM' / browsers 16 or 48 kHz PCM
claims.found: L515: 'Telephony typically uses 8 kHz mono µ-law or A-law PCM, while browsers and mobile devices' L516: 'commonly support 16 or 48 kHz PCM.'

claims.claim: Deepgram: 'format alignment. Avoid transcoding... its TTS can emit mu-law audio directly consumable by telephony gateways. Eliminating format conversion removes unnecessary latency and reduces failure modes'
claims.found: L719-723: 'The most important optimization is format alignment. Avoid transcoding wherever possible. ... its TTS can emit µ-law audio directly consumable by telephony gateways. Eliminating format conversion removes unnecessary latency and reduces failure modes.'

claims.claim: Modal's Kokoro accepts phonetic input and streams PCM; resampling at the WebRTC transport
claims.found: L35: 'TTS: KokoroTTS (82M). ... streaming output minimizes TTFB ... Accepts phonetic symbols as input'; L40: 'Client <-> Bot: WebRTC'. ('streams PCM' / 'resampling at WebRTC transport' are mild extrapolations not explicitly stated but consistent.)

summary: All 5 code_refs to TTS request-format and Twilio/utils mu-law conversion are SUPPORTED at the cited lines. Both Deepgram quotes (L514-516, L719-723) are verbatim-accurate. The one defect: the Rapida mechanism chain 'ProcessOutputAudio -> ConvertOutput -> g711 lib' is MISCITED — ConvertOutput uses resampler.Resample for mu-law encoding, while the g711 lib (zaf/g711) is only on the MixAmbient ambient path, not the main output path. No WRONG claims; overall PARTIAL.
